title: Lesson 393
transcript: All right, let's talk about physical segmentation. Of course, we're focused on domain number four here, which is all about network security. Physically segmenting your network is one of the best ways to provide security. And what this section really deals with is the management of the network and some of the options that you would have if you are physically segmented, for example. And so typical terminology that we talk about here is what you see on this slide. First, we have what's called in-band management. And the key word here is obviously in-band. The management of the network itself and its functions and so on is part of the network and on the same network itself. All right. So managing the network is done through the same network that all of the services and transmissions and everything else is happening. That's in-band. Now we say that this is less secure because unauthorized users may have access, because they have access to the network and its services, may also have access to the capability to be able to manage that network and therefore interfere with it. What's better from a security perspective is what's called out of band. Now we did talk about out of band in some other contexts like key distribution. in cryptography for example and we said that's a totally different channel that is not available to the attacker and so that makes it a really good way to distribute keys for example. It's the same concept here. You have a separate network that is used to manage the other network, for example, the main network. And so this is more secure because unauthorized users would not have access to the management network because there is really no physical connection between that and the actual network itself. And there's even a better option, in fact, when we talk about this, and that is what we call air-gapped networks. This is where there is no connection possible. Everything is air-gapped so that it stands alone. There is no physical or logical way to connect to that network which makes it the most secure. So there is no way to physically connect to it or even wirelessly connect to it. This is the most secure way. to set up physical segmentation because there is no possibility for anything external from that network to be able to connect to it.
